skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through the inference.sh infsh CLI.
skill.summary: Run Nano Banana 2 image generation and editing from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly Markdown examples, not Ruby execution or command injection. The skill still depends on the infsh CLI and sends prompts or images to external inference services, so it should publish with a network and external command warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run the infsh CLI for login, app execution, samples, and related skill installs. This is expected behavior, but it executes local commands and depends on a third-party tool.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in Markdown examples and match the allowed infsh tool scope. I found no evidence of shell metacharacter injection, but command execution is required for the skill.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Matches Are Markdown
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across Markdown code fences and inline parameter names. Manual review found documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are in SKILL.md documentation and include fenced Bash, Python examples, or inline code labels. Some examples run infsh, but the Ruby backtick execution classification is false.

low_findings.title: External Network Service Usage
low_findings.description: The skill uses inference.sh, remote documentation links, optional remote image URLs, and Google Search grounding. This can send prompts, images, and generated content to external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network dependency is stated directly in the skill and examples. This is legitimate for image generation, but users should understand the data sharing boundary.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain model names, table headings, or descriptive text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no cryptographic algorithm usage. The high-severity static matches appear to be keyword collisions in prose and Markdown tables.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Scope Allows infsh Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash execution for infsh commands. This is necessary for the skill, but marketplace users should review infsh commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration directly grants infsh command execution. The scope is limited to infsh rather than arbitrary shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Image Inputs May Be Fetched
dangerous_patterns.description: The image editing example accepts remote image URLs. This may cause external services to retrieve user-provided URLs and process their contents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited example includes a remote image URL in the input image list. The exact fetching behavior belongs to the external inference service, so the risk is contextual.

content.user_title: Create Gemini Images with Nano Banana 2
content.value_statement: Image teams need fast generation and editing without switching tools. This skill routes prompts and image inputs through inference.sh to Gemini Flash Image Preview.
actual_capabilities: Runs text-to-image generation with Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview.
Edits images using input image URLs or files supported by inference.sh.
Requests multiple image outputs from one prompt.
Sets aspect ratio and resolution options for generated images.
Enables Google Search grounding for current information prompts.
Shows Python SDK usage for direct app execution and streaming updates.
limitations: Requires an installed inference.sh CLI and an authenticated infsh session.
Sends prompts and image inputs to external inference providers.
Output quality depends on the remote model and service availability.
Does not add extra content safety checks beyond provider behavior.

use_cases.title: Rapid Concept Art
use_cases.description: Generate several visual directions from a written brief with size and style controls.

use_cases.title: Marketing Image Variants
use_cases.description: Create campaign visuals in multiple aspect ratios for social, web, and presentation use.

use_cases.title: Product Mockup Editing
use_cases.description: Modify existing reference images with targeted prompt instructions and grounded context.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to create one image of [subject] in [style] with [lighting] and [mood].

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Directions
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate four image concepts for [project]. Use [aspect ratio], [visual style], and distinct compositions.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this input image and change [specific element]. Preserve [important details] and output a [resolution] result.

prompt_templates.title: Ground a Current Visual
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image about [current topic] with Google Search grounding enabled. Show [visual interpretation] in [format].

output_examples.input: A prompt asks for a photorealistic banana floating above Earth at golden hour.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a generated image and a short model description of the scene.

output_examples.input: A user provides a landscape image and asks to add a rainbow in the sky.
output_examples.output: The skill returns an edited image that preserves the landscape while adding the requested rainbow.

output_examples.input: A team requests four logo concepts for a coffee shop in a minimalist style.
output_examples.output: The skill returns multiple generated image options for review and selection.

best_practices: Review each infsh command before running it, especially commands with file paths or URLs.
Use precise prompts that name subject, style, composition, lighting, and output size.
Avoid sending private images or confidential campaign material to external services.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts when brand, style, or composition must be controlled.
Do not assume generated images are licensed for every commercial use without review.
Do not enable Google Search grounding when the request does not need current information.

faq.question: What does this skill use to generate images?
faq.answer: It uses the inference.sh infsh CLI to run Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview.

faq.question: Does it work in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents image editing with input images and a text instruction.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends requests to inference.sh and may use Google Search grounding when enabled.

faq.question: Can it generate several images at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented num_images option requests multiple image outputs.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It appears publishable with warnings about external commands, network use, and third-party data processing.
